meta.name: Finland
meta.aboutStudying: Finland has one of the world's best education systems and EU students study for free. Finnish universities are research powerhouses, especially in technology, design and education science. The country ranks as the happiest in the world and offers a uniquely safe, clean and nature-rich environment for students.

tips.title: Free for EU Students
tips.text: EU/EEA students pay no tuition fees at Finnish universities. Non-EU students pay €8,000–18,000/year but generous scholarships cover 50–100% for strong applicants. The Finnish student union (KELA) also unlocks big discounts on meals, transport and healthcare.

tips.title: Nature & Sauna
tips.text: Finland is 75% forest and has 188,000 lakes. Saunas are a way of life — there are more saunas than cars. Winters are dark and snowy (perfect for the Northern Lights), while summers bring the magical midnight sun. Embrace the outdoors and you'll thrive.

tips.title: Tech & Design Leader
tips.text: Finland is the home of Nokia, Linux, Angry Birds and a thriving startup scene (Slush is Europe's biggest startup event). It's also a global design icon — Marimekko, Iittala, Alvar Aalto. English is spoken almost universally, so language is rarely a barrier.

universities.name: University of Helsinki
universities.desc: Finland's oldest and highest-ranked university — a global top-100 institution and a research powerhouse across all disciplines.

universities.name: Aalto University
universities.desc: Finland's flagship for technology, business and art & design — formed from three top schools and a global leader in design thinking.

universities.name: Tampere University
universities.desc: A broad research university combining technology and health sciences in Finland's biggest inland city.

universities.name: University of Turku
universities.desc: A leading multidisciplinary university in Finland's former capital, with a strong medical and biosciences focus.

universities.name: University of Oulu
universities.desc: One of the northernmost universities in the world and a global leader in wireless communications research.

universities.name: University of Jyväskylä
universities.desc: Finland's leading university for education science and sport, set on a beautiful Alvar Aalto-designed campus.

universities.name: LUT University
universities.desc: A technical and business university focused on clean energy and sustainability, near the Russian border.

universities.name: Åbo Akademi University
universities.desc: Finland's only Swedish-language university — a small, international research institution with a strong sense of community.

Helsinki.desc: Finland's design-forward capital on the Baltic Sea — clean, safe and surrounded by islands, with the country's top university and a buzzing startup scene.
Helsinki.studentLife: Excellent — large international community, free entry to many museums, sauna culture.
Helsinki.transport: Superb metro, trams and ferries; airport 30 min by train.
Helsinki.nightlife: Lively in summer with terraces and island parties; cosy bars in winter.

Espoo.desc: Helsinki's high-tech neighbour and home to Aalto University — a green, modern city that is the heart of Finland's technology and startup ecosystem.
Espoo.studentLife: Dynamic and entrepreneurial — Otaniemi is Finland's startup nucleus.
Espoo.transport: Metro extension links directly to central Helsinki in 20 minutes.
Espoo.nightlife: Quieter than Helsinki but the capital is a short metro ride away.

Tampere.desc: Finland's vibrant inland city set between two lakes — a former industrial hub turned cultural and student capital, consistently voted the most popular Finnish city to live in.
Tampere.studentLife: Outstanding — a large, friendly student population and famously sociable atmosphere.
Tampere.transport: New tram network; 1.5 hrs by train to Helsinki.
Tampere.nightlife: Excellent and affordable — one of Finland's best live music scenes.

Turku.desc: Finland's oldest city and former capital, beautifully set on the Aura River and gateway to the stunning archipelago — home to two universities and a rich medieval heritage.
Turku.studentLife: Strong — two universities create a lively, river-centred student culture.
Turku.transport: 2 hrs by train to Helsinki; ferries to Sweden from the harbour.
Turku.nightlife: Famous for its riverboat bars in summer; lively year-round.

cityCards.desc: Finland's design-forward Baltic capital — clean, safe and home to the country's top university.
Helsinki's high-tech neighbour and home to Aalto University — the heart of Finland's startup scene.
Finland's vibrant lake city — a former mill hub turned the most popular city to live in.
Finland's oldest city and archipelago gateway — medieval heritage and two universities.
